channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Federal Reserve holds a key interest rate steady amid growing economic pressures
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady today. The central bank is wrestling with a lot of uncertainty with a weak job market and stubborn inflation.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady today. The central bank is wrestling with a lot of uncertainty with a weak job market and stubborn inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The Fed holds interest rates steady as the economy faces deep uncertainty
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady as it faces inflationary pressure from the war with Iran — and a weakening labor market.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady as it faces inflationary pressure from the war with Iran — and a weakening labor market.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The war in Iran is impacting Earth's supply of helium
item.description: Helium is the second most abundant element in the universe, but on earth it's in short supply. And Iran's retaliatory strikes on helium-rich Qatar have made it even more scarce.
item.content:encoded: Helium is the second most abundant element in the universe, but on earth it's in short supply. And Iran's retaliatory strikes on helium-rich Qatar have made it even more scarce.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Energy analyst discusses the impact of the fuel shortage across industries in the U.S.
item.description: The war with Iran has caused diesel prices to spike nationwide. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with energy analyst Patrick De Haan about the implications for all the industries that rely on the fuel.
item.content:encoded: The war with Iran has caused diesel prices to spike nationwide. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with energy analyst Patrick De Haan about the implications for all the industries that rely on the fuel.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Fixing the toxic work culture of restaurants
item.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with restaurant industry veterans Kenji Lopez-Alt and Hannah Selinger about ways to fix the toxic work culture in many restaurant kitchens.
item.content:encoded: Host Adrian Ma speaks with restaurant industry veterans Kenji Lopez-Alt and Hannah Selinger about ways to fix the toxic work culture in many restaurant kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Paramount, Warner Bros. and regulators
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Columbia Law professor Tim Wu who makes the case for what he sees as the weak spots in the Paramount Warner-Bros. merger.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Columbia Law professor Tim Wu who makes the case for what he sees as the weak spots in the Paramount Warner-Bros. merger.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Judge blocks DOJ's criminal probe of Federal Reserve, blasting it as political
item.description: A federal judge has put the brakes on a criminal probe of the Federal Reserve, saying it was part of an improper campaign by the Trump administration to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has put the brakes on a criminal probe of the Federal Reserve, saying it was part of an improper campaign by the Trump administration to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Bucking stigma, more places turn to factory-built for affordable housing
item.description: Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.
item.content:encoded:
Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.

(Image credit: Anusha Mathur)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Egg prices have taken a beating. What's behind the drop?
item.description: A year ago, eggs were scarce and prices were sky-high. But avian flu took a much smaller toll on America's egg-laying chickens this winter than last, and egg prices have tumbled 42%.
item.content:encoded:
A year ago, eggs were scarce and prices were sky-high. But avian flu took a much smaller toll on America's egg-laying chickens this winter than last, and egg prices have tumbled 42%.

(Image credit: American Egg Board)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: China slams Trump's trade investigation, as it approves a 5-year economic plan
item.description: China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.
item.content:encoded:
China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak
